raw_text:
Approved. Your mapping preserves intent and the dependency DAG is correct.

Assignments (max throughput, minimal coupling)

CopperRiver → bd-3d8 (platform DB services + test wiring)
This unlocks bd-s6y and bd-q09, so it’s the critical path.

PurpleCliff → bd-13z (schema qualification normalization)
Independent, fast noise-reducer.

MaroonHarbor → bd-3d8 support OR pre-work for bd-s6y
If CopperRiver is deep in compose, have MaroonHarbor prep the readiness/NATS barrier utilities and logging changes in a branch, but do not merge until bd-3d8 lands.

Merge order

bd-13z can merge anytime once green.

bd-3d8 must merge next (infra foundation).

Then run:

bd-s6y (timeouts/readiness/NATS sync)

bd-q09 (envelope/metadata alignment)

Stop-the-line constraints (don’t violate)

No mocks/stubs added to make tests pass

Platform DBs must be explicit in compose (or explicit DBs on an existing PG service) and connected via env vars

If you choose “single PG service, multiple DBs”: document the env var mapping clearly and keep it deterministic

What I want back for the closure stamp

After all 4 beads are closed, paste:

cargo test -p e2e-tests --no-fail-fast summary (pass/fail counts)

A short note confirming platform DB containers exist in compose (names + ports)

Confirmation that the previously failing categories are now green (projection, tenant-registry, envelope)

Proceed.
